name: AWS CDK (Cloud Development Kit)
what_it_does: AWS Cloud Development Kit — defines AWS infrastructure using familiar programming languages (TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, C#) that synthesizes to CloudFormation templates. Provides high-level constructs (L1/L2/L3) that encode AWS best practices. No separate REST API — the CDK CLI synthesizes and deploys via CloudFormation. The CDK Pipelines construct enables CI/CD self-mutation.
use_cases: Defining AWS infrastructure in TypeScript or Python alongside application code
Reusable infrastructure constructs as library packages published to npm or PyPI
CI/CD pipelines that deploy AWS infrastructure via CDK Pipelines
Translating existing CloudFormation templates to higher-level CDK constructs
Building opinionated internal infrastructure patterns using L3 constructs
not_for: Multi-cloud infrastructure (CDK is AWS-only; use Pulumi or Terraform for multi-cloud)
Teams not using AWS at all
Agents that need to programmatically invoke infrastructure changes at runtime — CDK is a build-time tool, not a runtime API
Simple single-resource deployments where the AWS console or CLI suffices
best_when: Your team is AWS-only and wants to define infrastructure in TypeScript or Python with full IDE support, type checking, and reusable construct libraries, and you are comfortable with CloudFormation as the underlying execution engine.
avoid_when: You need multi-cloud support, or need agents to dynamically create infrastructure at runtime via API calls (CDK is a code synthesis tool, not a runtime API — use Pulumi Automation API instead).

auth.notes: CDK uses standard AWS credential chain (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, IAM roles, instance profiles, SSO). The synthesize step requires read access to resolve context values; the deploy step requires CloudFormation and target service permissions. CDK Pipelines use IAM roles for cross-account deployments. Least-privilege CDK deployment roles are recommended.

pricing.free_tier_limits: CDK itself is free and open source (Apache 2.0). You pay for AWS services deployed.
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers for CDK itself. AWS CloudFormation is free for the first 1,000 handler operations/month, then $0.0009/handler operation.
pricing.notes: CDK is free. Cost comes from AWS resources provisioned. CloudFormation execution is nearly free. The cdk deploy command is the deployment mechanism — no separate service fees.

performance.rate_limits: CDK synthesis is local and has no API rate limits. Deployment speed is bounded by CloudFormation execution (typically minutes to tens of minutes for complex stacks).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CDK synthesis errors are TypeScript/Python type errors and are quite clear when constructs are misconfigured. CloudFormation deployment errors are less friendly — they surface stack events that require parsing rollback messages. ROLLBACK_COMPLETE states require manual diagnosis of the failed resource event.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CloudFormation stacks are fully idempotent — running cdk deploy twice with no changes is a no-op. CDK diff shows what will change before deployment. Drift detection available via CloudFormation.
known_agent_gotchas: CDK is a synthesis tool, not a runtime API — agents cannot call CDK to provision resources dynamically at runtime without forking a subprocess
cdk bootstrap must be run in each AWS account/region before first deployment — a common setup oversight
CloudFormation stack updates use change sets — some changes require resource replacement (destructive), not just update
Stack naming collisions cause deployment failures — agents generating stack names must ensure uniqueness per account/region
CDK context values are cached in cdk.context.json — agents in CI must manage or clear this file appropriately
Lambda asset bundling (Docker-based) requires Docker running on the agent host
CDK Aspects run at synthesis time, not deploy time — agents cannot apply aspects dynamically after synthesis

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM integration is first-class — CDK has grant() methods on constructs for least-privilege permissions. CDK Nag and CDK Aspects enable policy-as-code security checks at synthesis. No secrets stored in CDK — uses AWS Secrets Manager and SSM Parameter Store lookups at deploy time. CDK constructs can inadvertently create overly permissive IAM roles if L2 convenience methods are used without review.
